deck.title: Electronics — Ohm's Law & Power
deck.description: V = IR, P = IV, and the unit conversions you'll see on the ASVAB EI subtest.

cards.front: Identify the unit: what does a volt (V) measure?
cards.explanation: The volt is the SI unit of electric potential and electromotive force; symbol V.

cards.front: What does the symbol Ω represent?
cards.back: The ohm — the unit of electrical resistance
cards.explanation: Ω (capital Greek omega) is the SI symbol for ohms; 1 Ω = 1 V/A.
V in volts, I in amps, R in ohms. Rearranged: I = V/R and R = V/I.

cards.front: Write the formula for electrical power in terms of current and voltage.
cards.explanation: Power equals current multiplied by voltage; the unit is the watt (W).

cards.front: What does an ammeter measure, and in what unit?
cards.explanation: Current is the rate of charge flow; 1 amp = 1 coulomb per second.

cards.front: Convert: 250 mA equals how many amps?
cards.explanation: Milli- means one-thousandth, so 1 mA = 0.001 A; 250 × 0.001 = 0.25 A.

cards.front: Calculate: 12 V is applied across a 4 Ω resistor. What is the current?
A 60 W bulb runs on 120 V. How much current does it draw?
Convert: 2.2 kΩ equals how many ohms?
cards.explanation: Kilo- means one thousand, so 1 kΩ = 1,000 Ω; 2.2 × 1,000 = 2,200 Ω.

cards.front: Write the power formula that uses current and resistance only.
cards.explanation: Substitute V = IR into P = IV to get P = I²R; useful when V is unknown.

cards.front: Two 10 Ω resistors are wired in series. What is the total resistance?
cards.explanation: In series, resistances add: R_total = R1 + R2 = 10 + 10 = 20 Ω.

cards.front: Calculate the power dissipated by a 5 Ω resistor carrying 2 A.
A 240 V source connects to a 60 Ω heater. How many watts does it dissipate?
A 9 V battery pushes 30 mA through a resistor. What is the resistor's value?
cards.explanation: Convert 30 mA = 0.03 A, then R = V/I = 9 / 0.03 = 300 Ω.

cards.front: Two 10 Ω resistors are wired in parallel across 20 V. What is the total current from the source?
